headline: Sheets aligns with your economic priorities on combining market reforms with public investment, though his AI stance includes more caution than your minimal-regulation preference.
agreements: Sheets supports a mix of market freedom and targeted public investment based on your stated priority: he has called for supply-side structural reforms (labor-market flexibility, deregulation, privatization) alongside active fiscal and monetary stimulus when demand is weak, arguing both sides must be balanced (public statement, Treasury remarks).

On AI and technology, Sheets partially aligns with your minimal-regulation preference: he has expressed optimism about AI's productivity potential and generally favors market-driven innovation over heavy-handed intervention, though he has also voiced caution about market valuations (public statement, Duke address).
caveat: Your situation as an uninsured homeowner does not intersect with any of the two issues where evidence was found; key concerns like healthcare access and housing costs fall among the unknown issues.
evidence_note: Evidence consists entirely of public statements from Sheets' time at Treasury and a university address, with moderate to high confidence on economy but lower confidence on AI due to limited sourcing.
